text: Tabletop exercise to rehearse OOM scenarios against Dedicated pools and Hosted fallbacks ahead of the planned model rollout. We ran through simulated failure modes, prioritization for mitigation playbooks, and assigned short-term operational runbooks and longer-term infra tasks. Key outcome: sequence of tactical checkpoints (stress tests, improved observability, updated canary abort criteria) and explicit owners for each contingency step.

text:
Header:
Date: 2026-03-18
Start: 11:00 UTC
Duration: ~75 minutes
Attendees: Ava Chen, Mateo Alvarez, Priya Nair, Liam O'Connor, Jordan Blake, Elena Park, Noah Wright, Sana Mir, Greg Foster

Summary:
Quick internal workshop to align on a concrete 'capacity staircase' (phased ramps), explicit rollback criteria for model and capacity regressions, and a short playbook for dry-run/drill execution. Focus was on launch readiness for the next private deployment wave and a plan to avoid cost surprises while keeping SLOs.

Topics:
- Capacity staircase definition (levels, triggers)
- Canary and dry-run plan
- Rollback criteria: quality regressions vs infra regressions
- Observability needs and runbook edits
- Security/audit notes for private deployments

Action items (captured live): see metadata section above.

Transcript body:
[00:00] Ava: alright, uh thanks everyone for squeezing this in, I kn...

title: Launch gating and incident ownership sprint sync
preview: Planning sync to finalize launch gating criteria for next beta, review last weekend incident postmortem, assign incident ownership changes, and finalize preflight checklist for model onboarding.
text: Planning sync to finalize launch gating criteria for next beta, review last weekend incident postmortem, assign incident ownership changes, and finalize preflight checklist for model onboarding.

title: Known-issues sprint publish review (workarounds + 'when to escalate')
preview: Internal publish-review for the Known-Issues Sprint. Team did a rapid pass over five workaround articles (streaming disconnects/timeouts, 429 quota mismatch, Dedicated autoscaling oscillation, Private installer upgrade stuck at migrations, audit log delivery latency) to ensure they are copy/paste-ready for Support, ...
text: Internal publish-review for the Known-Issues Sprint. Team did a rapid pass over five workaround articles (streaming disconnects/timeouts, 429 quota mismatch, Dedicated autoscaling oscillation, Private installer upgrade stuck at migrations, audit log delivery latency) to ensure they are copy/paste-ready for Support, include clear escalation criteria, and avoid customer-sensitive details. Agreed on a consistent 'When to escalate' rubric mapped to Support P0/P1 triggers, added owners/reviewers per article, and aligned on what must be linked from Support macros and the known-issues index before merging docs PRs.

title: Support + SRE playbook alignment (escalation triggers, bridges, update cadence)
preview: Support leadership and SRE aligned on (1) what constitutes P0/P1 from Support vs Sev0/Sev1 in incident terms, (2) concrete paging/escalation triggers (error-rate/latency/customer-count/revenue risk), (3) Slack bridge conventions and when to move from #support bridge into #incidents, and (4) update cadence language f...
text: Support leadership and SRE aligned on (1) what constitutes P0/P1 from Support vs Sev0/Sev1 in incident terms, (2) concrete paging/escalation triggers (error-rate/latency/customer-count/revenue risk), (3) Slack bridge conventions and when to move from #support bridge into #incidents, and (4) update cadence language for enterprise vs non-enterprise (and how to handle “Waiting on Customer” without gaming the SLA). The group agreed to standardize a short “page template” (request IDs, region, model, recent deploys, customer tier, and what changed), require linking the SUP Jira ticket in the bridge, and to use a consistent cadence phrased as targets rather than guarantees. Follow-ups include edits to the Confluence SLA table wording, Jira automation for required fields + SLA timers, and incident-bot improvements to pin SUP ticket links in bridge channels.

title: Marketplace onboarding walkthrough (internal dry run) — subscription → org provisioning → first key
preview: Internal walkthrough of the end-to-end marketplace onboarding flow to validate the UX and docs before submission. Team identified confusion around org linking for existing Redwood orgs, unclear messaging during entitlement propagation delays (AWS especially), and missing docs for the "already subscribed" and "multip...
text: Internal walkthrough of the end-to-end marketplace onboarding flow to validate the UX and docs before submission. Team identified confusion around org linking for existing Redwood orgs, unclear messaging during entitlement propagation delays (AWS especially), and missing docs for the "already subscribed" and "multiple subscriptions" edge cases. Engineering confirmed expected entitlement API error codes and noted where console should map them to actionable retry guidance. Docs team flagged needed screenshots and a clearer separation of AWS vs GCP steps, plus a single canonical "How marketplace billing maps to usage" section to reduce support tickets.

title: Lifecycle growth sprint — retargeting launch checklist
preview: Internal working session to finalize retargeting launch details for the lifecycle growth experiments sprint: audience definitions and suppression rules (active opps + enterprise domains), UTM naming conventions, landing page mapping, creative variant set, spend caps, and measurement expectations. Team aligned on a c...
text: Internal working session to finalize retargeting launch details for the lifecycle growth experiments sprint: audience definitions and suppression rules (active opps + enterprise domains), UTM naming conventions, landing page mapping, creative variant set, spend caps, and measurement expectations. Team aligned on a conservative ramp, a hard exclusion list refresh cadence, and how to interpret activation vs retention signals for retargeting.

title: Weekly Growth Experiments Readout (Lifecycle sprint) - Jan 21
preview: Team reviewed early Week 1 results for onboarding email v2 A/B (opens/clicks and preliminary activation lift), agreed to keep test running through end of week while fixing a small tracking discrepancy. Product/Eng walked through Week 2 console nudge rollout: targeted checklist-style nudges (API key creation + first ...
text: Team reviewed early Week 1 results for onboarding email v2 A/B (opens/clicks and preliminary activation lift), agreed to keep test running through end of week while fixing a small tracking discrepancy. Product/Eng walked through Week 2 console nudge rollout: targeted checklist-style nudges (API key creation + first request) behind feature flags with a holdout group, plus QA and dismissal behavior fixes before ramping exposure.

title: Lifecycle growth experiments sprint kickoff (Hosted API activation/retention)
preview: Kickoff for 4-week lifecycle growth experiments sprint focused on self-serve Hosted API activation + early retention. Team aligned on primary activation KPIs (first successful request + first 100k tokens within 7 days), experiment tracks (email drip v2, in-console nudges, paid retargeting), instrumentation/event tax...
text: Kickoff for 4-week lifecycle growth experiments sprint focused on self-serve Hosted API activation + early retention. Team aligned on primary activation KPIs (first successful request + first 100k tokens within 7 days), experiment tracks (email drip v2, in-console nudges, paid retargeting), instrumentation/event taxonomy, and guardrails (deliverability, CAN-SPAM/GDPR, sales opp exclusions, and experiment integrity). Owners confirmed and week-by-week deliverables reviewed.

title: Regional failover automation upgrade - kickoff
preview: Kickoff for the Reliability Program workstream to upgrade regional failover automation across Hosted API and Dedicated. Team aligned on problem statement (manual/partial automation, noisy triggers, slow detection, hard to validate), in-scope components (signal pipeline, region health aggregation, controller + determ...
text: Kickoff for the Reliability Program workstream to upgrade regional failover automation across Hosted API and Dedicated. Team aligned on problem statement (manual/partial automation, noisy triggers, slow detection, hard to validate), in-scope components (signal pipeline, region health aggregation, controller + deterministic policy evaluation, guardrails/rollback, observability, runbooks, game days) and out-of-scope items (multi-cloud migration, serving runtime rewrite, SLA language). Open questions focused on trigger hierarchy (authoritative signals vs corroboration), anti-flap behavior, approval model (auto vs ack-required), and how to safely run controlled production failovers with limited blast radius.

title: Incident taxonomy working session (v1) - tricky classifications + owner mapping
preview: Working session to finalize incident taxonomy v1 edges and the owner mapping for disputed categories. Team aligned on keeping top-level types small (target ~12) with subtypes where needed. Resolved key disputes: capacity shortage remains Fleet primary; routing/fallback misbehavior remains Smart Routing primary; quot...
text: Working session to finalize incident taxonomy v1 edges and the owner mapping for disputed categories. Team aligned on keeping top-level types small (target ~12) with subtypes where needed. Resolved key disputes: capacity shortage remains Fleet primary; routing/fallback misbehavior remains Smart Routing primary; quota/rate limit enforcement is Control Plane primary (Support consulted for customer-caused throttling); streaming disconnects default to Edge/Gateway unless clear client SDK regression. Agreed to add a simple rule for multi-cause incidents: pick the mitigation owner as primary type, track other systems as contributing factors in postmortem. Observability reiterated cardinality guardrails (allowlist + unknown bucket). Action items captured for updating the decision tree, owner mapping config, and incident-bot suggestions.

title: Post-launch retro - Benchmark report campaign (KPI review + refresh plan)
preview: Team reviewed performance of the public benchmark report launch (landing page + interactive table + PDF + blog + webinar). Results were strong top-of-funnel with meaningful sales-assisted pipeline influence, but launch-day reliability issues (cache miss storm + 429s) created support load and some negative chatter. T...
text: Team reviewed performance of the public benchmark report launch (landing page + interactive table + PDF + blog + webinar). Results were strong top-of-funnel with meaningful sales-assisted pipeline influence, but launch-day reliability issues (cache miss storm + 429s) created support load and some negative chatter. The methodology page and reproducibility artifacts were consistently cited as the trust driver. Next phase focuses on (1) tightening follow-up loops for high-intent leads, (2) shipping a lightweight “replicate this in your PoC” guide and a clearer metric explainer, (3) improving cache/CDN settings and guardrails for future spikes, and (4) establishing a refresh cadence with clear material-change thresholds and legal review gates.

text: Marissa and the team walked through the internal Unit Economics dashboard v1 in Console, focusing on the primary KPI (blended $/1M output tokens) and how to use filters and drilldowns to find unit-cost outliers by model/version, region, customer tier, quantization profile, and streaming vs non-streaming. Logan/Nadia covered metering and aggregation basics and the current data-quality gates. Rishi/Laura discussed Finance expectations for reconciliation and stability when the GPU rate card updates. The group reviewed known limitations (remaining streaming parity edge cases, region normalization ‘unknown’ bucket, dedicated/private tier edge cases, and how to interpret idle/overhead allocation) and agreed on next steps for v1 launch readiness and v1.1 backlog items.
